evals.prompt: Here are my finances: checking $3,000, savings $12,000, 401k $68,000, car value $15,000. Debts: car loan $9,000, student loan $22,000, credit card $3,500. What's my net worth and how healthy does it look?
evals.expected_output: Total assets $98,000; total liabilities $34,500; net worth $63,500; debt-to-asset ratio 35.2% (healthy, below 50%); liquid assets $15,000 (15.3%); 401k is 69% of assets — concentrated but age-appropriate for a wealth-builder.
assertions: Provides health commentary on the ratios

evals.prompt: My net worth last year was $45,000. This year it's $63,500. What's my year-over-year growth and am I building wealth fast enough for someone in their 30s?
evals.expected_output: Dollar change: +$18,500. Percentage change: +41.1%. This is strong growth. A common benchmark (Millionaire Next Door formula) suggests net worth of Age × Gross Income / 10. Growth of $18,500/year is solid for a wealth-building phase.
assertions: Benchmarks the result against a rule-of-thumb or percentile
Provides context on whether the rate is on track

evals.prompt: From the attached balance sheet (evals/files/balance_sheet.csv), calculate net worth, debt-to-asset ratio, and asset allocation. Compare to the prior period values in the file.
evals.expected_output: Current net worth $87,500; prior net worth $74,200; change +$13,300 (+17.9%); debt-to-asset ratio 28.6%; retirement accounts 71% of total assets. Primary driver: retirement account growth.
assertions: Loads both current and prior period data from CSV
Identifies retirement accounts as largest asset class
